ddls:
CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "data_type_enum" TEXT /* Enumeration for data type (e.g., adBigInt, adBoolean) */,
  "size" INTEGER /* Size of the data type in bytes */,
  "access" TEXT /* Access level (e.g., YesNo, None) */,
  "sql_server_type" TEXT /* SQL Server representation of the data type */,
  "oracle_type" TEXT /* Oracle representation of the data type */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the data type */,
  "allowed_values" TEXT /* List of allowed values */,
  "is_nullable" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the data type can be null */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the data type */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data type was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data type was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_mappings" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the corresponding data type */,
  "database_system" TEXT /* Name of the database system (e.g., SQL Server, Oracle) */,
  "mapping_description" TEXT /* Description of the mapping */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether this is the default mapping */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the mapping was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the mapping was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_mappings_data_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_type_id") REFERENCES data_types ("data_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_levels" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access level */,
  "access_name" TEXT /* Name of the access level (e.g., None, YesNo) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the access level */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether this is the default access level */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access level was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access level was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was last updated */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data type affected */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was performed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user who made the change */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client used to make the change */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the log entry was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "validation_rules" (
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each validation rule */,
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data type this rule applies to */,
  "rule_description" TEXT /* Description of the validation rule */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the rule is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the rule was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the rule was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_validation_rules_data_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_type_id") REFERENCES data_types ("data_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, developer) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Description of permissions associated with this role */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether this is the default role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_conversions" (
  "conversion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each conversion rule */,
  "from_data_type" INTEGER /* ID of the source data type */,
  "to_data_type" INTEGER /* ID of the target data type */,
  "conversion_method" TEXT /* Method used for conversion */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether this is the default conversion */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the conversion was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the conversion was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("conversion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_conversions_to_data_type FOREIGN KEY ("to_data_type") REFERENCES data_types ("data_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_conversions_from_data_type FOREIGN KEY ("from_data_type") REFERENCES data_types ("data_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_type_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version entry */,
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data type this version represents */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the data type */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of changes made in this version */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether this is the current version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the version was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the version was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_type_versions_data_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_type_id") REFERENCES data_types ("data_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_logs" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the error */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Description of the error encountered */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error occurred */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user associated with the error */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client where the error occurred */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error log was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
